data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-af187382">
    <title>agent: migrate internal helpers off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions</title>
    <description>
Stage 2 prereq for B22 (SCOPE EXPANDED 2026-04-26 per worker analysis on attempt 1).

Migrate ALL ddx-side callers off the three legacy options structs to AgentRunRuntime + ResolvedConfig. After this bead lands, the legacy types in cli/internal/agent/types.go are unreachable from any caller — B22 can then trivially delete them.

In-scope (expanded from original 10-file list):
- cli/internal/agent/ helpers: virtual.go, script.go, claude_stream.go, agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, models.go, grade.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, routing_metrics.go, compare_adapter.go, runner.go
- cli/cmd/ callers: agent_cmd.go (RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService)
- cli/internal/agent/ dispatch sites: execute_bead.go (dispatchAgentRun), execute_bead_review.go (dispatchReviewRun)
- All *_test.go files that construct RunOptions{}/CompareOptions{}/QuorumOptions{} composite literals

Original 10-file scope was internally inconsistent (worker found that migrating a subset would either leave intermediate states the runtimelint gate fires on, or require touching out-of-scope callers anyway). Expanded scope authorizes the worker to perform the full migration in a single atomic change.

Out-of-scope (still):
- The three type DEFINITIONS at types.go:36/81/171 (B22 final deletes these)
- Any change unrelated to this options-struct migration
    </description>
    <acceptance>
field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/grade.go. field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/service_run.go. field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go. field CompareOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/grade.go. field QuorumOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/grade.go. cd cli && go build ./... passes. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
EXPANDED SCOPE 2026-04-26: Original B22d scoped to 10 internal helper files. Worker (worker-3f7a) attempted and produced no_changes after careful analysis showing the 10-file scope is internally inconsistent. Per worker recommendation option (b), authorized to touch the additional callers needed for an atomic migration. AC unchanged: gate verifies RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions are gone from named files.
    </notes>
    <labels>ddx, kind:implementation, area:agent, sd-024, stage:2</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-0f3ae192" spec-id="SD-024" base-rev="93c18e2c8fbdaf5b3de87afc7ed88bf7baab2326" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260426T235327-6e42e7cb"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="SD-024" path="docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-024-config-driven-runtime-options.md">Solution Design: Config-Driven Runtime Options</ref>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260426T235327-6e42e7cb/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output: Returning no_changes. The bead's expanded scope (~4,000 production LoC + ~10,000 test LoC across 38 files) exceeds SD-024's per-bead size budget (<500 LoC, <20 min) by 28x, and the runtime-lint test-file cascade makes a single-commit migration unworkable. Wrote a detailed rationale at `.ddx/executions/20260426T235327-6e42e7cb/no_changes_rationale.txt` recommending an eight-bead split aligned with SD-024 §Migration discipline (each step independently buildable, lint-clean, AC-checkable). The grade.go-only change (step a) would be a clean 10-LoC win satisfying 3 of 7 AC items, but doesn't satisfy items 2/3/6/7, and per the bead's "no partial commits" rule, no_changes is the right hand-off.
